content:
# Pressure Pulse Decay

**Summary**: A transient technique used to measure the permeability of low-permeability rocks by monitoring pressure changes over time.

---

## How It Is Used in the Thesis Collection

- [[2000-khan|Khan, 2000]] used pressure-pulse-decay to evaluate permeability in tight rock samples.

- [[2009-bajaalah|BAJAALAH, 2009]] used pressure- pulse decay to measure permeability of tight rocks and fractured core samples.

## Instruments and Software



- [[2009-bajaalah|BAJAALAH, 2009]] used pressure- pulse decay to measure permeability of tight rocks and fractured core samples.

## Typical Workflow



## Key Results and Findings

- The method is superior to conventional steady state methods for tight rocks as it is faster and easier to perform. ([[2000-khan|Khan, 2000, p. 18]])

- The technique is faster and easier than steady-state methods, making it suitable for low-permeability formations. ([[2009-bajaalah|BAJAALAH, 2009, p. 13]])
